result:
Fleshed the brief and wrote it to `/workspace/nexus-mk2/.scratch/patron-anima-eval/eval-a/outputs/patron-flesh/cli-flag-edit.md`.

Key calls I made:
- **Framed it as completing a set, not adding a feature** (#36) — title + body are the natural pair; shipping edit for one without the other is an incoherent half-surface.
- **Named a specific reader** (#22) — author/operator correcting a just-posted commission, low-per-writ but recurring.
- **Held scope tight** (#23) — no status/parent/assignee edits, no bulk, no `--from-file`. Just the missing sibling flag.
- **Fail-loud on "nothing to edit"** (#2) — errors rather than silently no-opping.
- **Routed through existing API, generalize the mutator** (#3, #5) — one patch-shaped mutator, two flags routing into it, rather than a title-specific parallel method.
- **Flagged three deferred questions** the planner should resolve before dispatch (writ types without titles, body-flag idempotence behavior to match, existing `retitle`-style commands).
